title: Remove Unused Private Methods
description: A method that is never called is considered dead code and should be removed. This rule detects methods that are never referenced from inside a translation unit, and cannot be referenced from the outside. It is important to note that this rule does not take reflection into account, which means that issues will be raised on private methods that are only accessed using the reflection API.
recommendation: Delete the unused private methods from your code. However, ensure that these methods are not being accessed using the reflection API before removal.
impact: Removing dead code decreases the size of the maintained codebase, making it easier to understand the program and preventing bugs from being introduced.
